The 14b types block does NOT include error_code in ReadinessRow or AuditReadinessSnapshot, but this is correct — error_code is on ValidationError (schemas.py), not on ReadinessRow. The types added in 14b (ReadinessRow, AuditReadinessSnapshot, AuditReadinessExplain, ReadinessRowId, ReadinessStatus) are the audit-readiness panel types, not ValidationError. The frontend ValidationError type in types/index.ts is not modified by this plan. However, the 14a schema co-update adds error_code to the BACKEND ValidationError. The frontend ValidationError type at types/index.ts:310-315 would need widening if the frontend consumes error_code directly. The plan does not consume error_code in any frontend-visible rendering — the ReadinessRow.component_ids pathway uses it only on the backend service layer. new_blocking_issues.title: ValidationCheck/ValidationError existing call sites NOT updated — No-Legacy policy violation
new_blocking_issues.evidence: The plan prescribes updating all existing construction sites of ValidationCheck() and ValidationError() in the SAME commit as adding the new required fields (affected_nodes, error_code). However, the codebase currently has multiple construction sites that WILL break when these fields become required (no defaults): src/elspeth/web/execution/validation.py:367-372 constructs ValidationCheck(name, passed, detail) — missing affected_nodes. Lines 585-598, 613-627, 633-637, 641-645 — same missing field. src/elspeth/web/execution/service.py:657-661 constructs ValidationCheck(name, passed, detail) — missing affected_nodes. src/elspeth/web/execution/validation.py:593-598, 621-627 construct ValidationError(component_id, component_type, message, suggestion) — missing error_code. src/elspeth/web/execution/service.py:664-670 constructs ValidationError(component_id, component_type, message, suggestion) — missing error_code. src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py:1071-1079 constructs both ValidationCheck and ValidationError without the new fields. src/elspeth/web/execution/validation.py:1242-1254 constructs ValidationCheck for identity_node_advisory WITHOUT affected_nodes (the advisory check is the one that needs it most — this is the specific production site that must supply affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,)). The plan prescribes a grep and co-update but the PLAN ITSELF does not enumerate all call sites that need updating. The implementer will run the grep and find 8+ call sites. Critically, the plan's spec at Task 1 Step 2b says 'audit all other ValidationCheck/ValidationError sites and add the new required fields explicitly' — this is correct intent. BUT the plan's commit message template (Task 1 Step 3) only lists execution/schemas.py and execution/validation.py in the git add. composer/service.py is missing from the git add list, meaning it won't be committed atomically. The identity_node_advisory site at validation.py:1242 also currently lacks affected_nodes (confirmed by reading current file state at lines 1242-1254 — the ValidationCheck there passes name, passed, detail but NOT affected_nodes).
new_blocking_issues.fix: Ensure the git add in Task 1 Step 3 includes src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py. Implementer must also update service.py:1071 (ValidationCheck) and service.py:1073-1079 (ValidationError) with the new fields. The validation.py:1242 identity advisory site must pass affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,) per the plan's spec — this site is the load-bearing one for the provenance row. Enumerate ALL construction sites explicitly in the plan rather than relying on the implementer to discover them from grep output.

The false claim remains unchanged from prior W2. Impact is documentation-only, not runtime.

The allowlist comments cite source files (e.g., 'plugins/transforms/web_scrape.py — Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL'). These verified results match the actual grep output confirmed during this review. The procedure is sound. Minor: the comment for azure_content_safety and azure_prompt_shield cites the base class path (azure/base.py:109) rather than the concrete class file, but the names are correct.

new_warnings.title: N2 — Task 1 Step 3 git add omits composer/service.py from the No-Legacy co-update commit
new_warnings.evidence: 14a Task 1 Step 3 git add lists: audit_readiness/__init__.py, audit_readiness/models.py, config.py, execution/schemas.py, execution/validation.py, tests/unit/web/audit_readiness/__init__.py, tests/unit/web/audit_readiness/test_models.py. Missing from this list: src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py which constructs ValidationCheck at line 1071 and ValidationError at lines 1073-1079. If the implementer follows the commit template literally, they will commit the required-field changes to schemas.py without updating service.py in the same commit, breaking the No-Legacy rule and breaking the service.py module at import time when the new fields are required.

new_warnings.title: N3 — state_from_record collaborator is legitimate DI, not test-injection seam
new_warnings.evidence: The ReadinessService.__init__ accepts state_from_record: Callable[..., CompositionState] | None = None with a default of the real converter. This is the same DI pattern used by execution/service.py (which imports state_from_record directly from sessions/converters.py). In tests, passing a lambda is standard. This is NOT a legacy shim — the production callers (app.py wiring) do not pass state_from_record, so it defaults to the real converter. Tests inject a lambda for isolation. No backdoor production path. CLAUDE.md's no-legacy rule applies to compatibility shims, not to constructor-injected collaborators.

new_warnings.title: N4 — AbortController monotonic write guard race analysis: correct design, one edge case
new_warnings.evidence: 14b Task 3 Step 3 implements AbortController abort-on-new-fetch + monotonic write guard (snapshot.composition_version > incoming). The guard correctly prevents stale slow responses from overwriting a faster newer response. Edge case: when v1 fetch is aborted (signal.aborted fires), the catch branch checks for AbortError by name — but fetch() with an aborted signal throws a DOMException with name 'AbortError', not a generic Error. The guard 'if ((err as { name?: string }).name === "AbortError") return;' correctly handles this. However: the abort fires BEFORE the fetch resolves in normal abort scenarios, but the test simulates a case where the fetch mock resolves AFTER the abort is signalled. In a real browser, an already-resolved promise from a mocked fetch will not throw AbortError on signal.aborted; the guard therefore falls to the monotonic write check. The monotonic write check catches the v1-after-v2 case correctly. The design is sound; the test's abort semantics use mock resolution rather than real abort, which is acceptable for unit tests.

new_warnings.title: N5 — canValidate/isValidating become dead code variables after Validate button removal
new_warnings.evidence: Task 8 Step 3 of 14c instructs deleting the Validate button block but retaining handleValidate, canValidate, isValidating, injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback. Once the button is removed, canValidate (line 381-386) and isValidating (line 361) have no rendering consumers — they are only used in the disabled/aria-label attributes of the now-deleted button and in handleValidate's guard (line 388). handleValidate is kept live but dead from a UI trigger perspective. Task 8 Step 5 prescribes: 'If canValidate or isValidating become unused after the button is removed, suppress eslint warnings with eslint-disable-next-line with a TODO comment.' This is correct. However, the plan instructs this for lint only — mypy/tsc will also complain about declared-but-unused vars in TypeScript. The plan says to run lint (eslint) but the variables are TypeScript, so the unused-variable warnings come from both eslint and tsc (via noUnusedLocals if enabled). If tsconfig.json enables noUnusedLocals, tsc will fail. The plan only addresses the eslint case.

new_warnings.title: N6 — EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_SINKS: dataverse sink confirmed; azure_blob sink absence is correct
new_warnings.evidence: EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_SINKS = frozenset({'dataverse'}) is correct. sinks/dataverse.py:227 has Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL. sinks/azure_blob_sink.py:307 uses BaseSink which has IO_WRITE determinism, not EXTERNAL_CALL — correctly excluded from the allowlist and the completeness test. sinks/chroma_sink.py was checked: chroma_sink does not declare Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL (not in the grep output). sinks/database_sink.py, json_sink.py, csv_sink.py — no EXTERNAL_CALL. The allowlist is complete for the current catalog.

notes.title: W5 Task 8.5 guard test: symbols verified real
notes.detail: subscriptions.handoff.test.ts imports initStoreSubscriptions from './subscriptions' — confirmed real at src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/stores/subscriptions.ts:20. notes.title: makeComposition fixture uses as-never cast for source field
notes.detail: 14b's makeComposition uses '{ kind: 'csv_file', config: { path: 'x.csv' } } as never' for the source field. This is a TypeScript test-only escape hatch, not a runtime compat shim. The backend NodeSpec (Python) is separate from the frontend TypeScript CompositionState.source shape. The 'as never' suppresses the type incompatibility in tests and is a standard testing pattern in this codebase.

summary.prior_blockers_resolved: 5/5 — all five blockers are substantively fixed
summary.prior_warnings_resolved: 5/7 — W1, W3, W5, W6, W7 fully fixed; W2 and W4 remain as warnings
blocking_summary: NB1: The No-Legacy co-update for ValidationCheck/ValidationError is correct in intent but the Task 1 Step 3 git add list omits composer/service.py. If the implementer follows the commit template literally, service.py:1071-1079 will not be updated in the same commit as the schema change — violating the CLAUDE.md No-Legacy rule and breaking the build. Additionally, validation.py:1242 (the identity_node_advisory site) is the most critical call site and must supply affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,) per the plan's own spec — the current file does not pass this argument. The plan prescribes the grep-and-update correctly but the commit template does not enforce completeness.
summary.most_critical_remaining_gap: NB1 — the git add in Task 1 Step 3 must be expanded to include src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py; the identity_node_advisory ValidationCheck at validation.py:1242 must receive affected_nodes in the same commit.
